message:
test(ft-conformance): linalg.svd numpy subprocess conformance [bd-c36b]

Adds torch_linalg_svd_numpy_subprocess_conformance covering 6
matrices: square 3x3, 4x4 identity, tall 4x2, wide 2x4, square
upper triangular 3x3, and 1x1 scalar.

U and Vh have per-column / per-row sign ambiguity (different LAPACK
builds flip signs) so element-wise comparison is fragile. The
harness instead verifies four structural invariants that uniquely
characterize a valid SVD:
  - S          ≈ S_numpy  (singular values are unique, sorted desc)
  - U @ diag(S) @ Vh ≈ A  (reconstruction; matches numpy)
  - U^T @ U   ≈ I_k       (column-orthonormality of U)
  - Vh @ Vh^T ≈ I_k       (row-orthonormality of Vh)

Surfaced one divergence in the process: FrankenTorch's SVD doesn't
complete the orthonormal basis on rank-deficient inputs (U columns
for zero singular values come back as all-zeros instead of unit
vectors orthogonal to the column space). Tracked under
frankentorch-zs8a; the rank-deficient 3x3 case is intentionally
omitted from this harness until the basis-completion bug is fixed.

Sister harness to the inv / solve / cholesky / qr / det / slogdet
harnesses. Files closure for the svd slice of frankentorch-c36b.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
